patient_question: I am being tested for kidney cancer
canonical_question: Which hospital should I go to for my type of cancer?
english_summary:
• Kidney cancer usually does not cause any symptoms in the beginning.
• A tumor in your kidney is often discovered by chance.
• As kidney cancer grows, you may experience one or more of these symptoms:
  ◦ blood in the urine
  ◦ pain in your side
  ◦ a lump in your abdomen that can be felt on the outside
• You will undergo examinations by the urologist.
• You choose a treatment together with your doctor.
english_full: Kidney cancer is a form of cancer in the kidneys. Cancer can develop in different places in the kidneys. The information on Thuisarts is about the most common type of kidney cancer. Doctors call this type of cancer renal cell cancer. 8 out of 10 people with kidney cancer have this type. In the beginning you almost never have any complaints from kidney cancer. It is often discovered by accident that you have a tumor in your kidney. For example, you have had an ultrasound or CT scan of your abdomen for another reason and the doctor sees a tumor in your kidney. If the kidney cancer grows, you may experience one or more of these symptoms: a lump in your abdomen that can be felt on the outside. Your GP or other doctor will refer you to a specialist in kidney diseases (urologist) for more examination. To investigate whether you have kidney cancer, you will receive a CT scan of your abdomen. This allows the urologist to see whether there is a tumor in your kidney and how large it is. If the tumor on your kidney is smaller than 4 centimeters, you will often also have an X-ray of your chest. This allows the doctor to see whether the tumor has spread. If the tumor is larger than 4 centimeters, you will often receive a CT scan of your chest. This makes it easier to see whether the tumor has spread to other organs, such as your lungs. The urologist will discuss with you what you want and what treatments are possible for you. This depends, for example, on: how large the tumor is in your kidney; whether you have any other diseases. If the tumor is smaller than 4 centimeters, the doctor may first remove a small piece of kidney (biopsy) to be examined in the laboratory. In the laboratory they can usually see whether the tumor is cancer. 3 out of 10 growths smaller than 4 centimeters are not cancer. If the doctors think it is not cancer, treatment is not always necessary. You will then receive regular checks to see whether the tumor is growing. You can still receive treatment later, if necessary. Sometimes they cannot clearly see in the laboratory whether it is cancer. Your doctor will discuss with you what best suits your situation. For example, removing a piece of kidney for examination, regular check-ups or treatment of the tumor.
original_dutch: Nierkanker is een vorm van kanker in de nieren. Kanker kan op verschillende plekken in de nieren ontstaan. De informatie op Thuisarts gaat over de soort nierkanker die het meest voorkomt. Artsen noemen deze soort kanker niercelkanker. 8 van de 10 mensen met nierkanker heeft deze soort. In het begin heeft u bijna nooit klachten bij nierkanker. Vaak wordt per toeval ontdekt dat u een gezwel in uw nier heeft. U heeft bijvoorbeeld een echo of CT-scan van uw buik gehad om een andere reden en de arts ziet een gezwel in uw nier. Als de nierkanker groeit, kunt u bijvoorbeeld 1 of meer van deze klachten krijgen: een bult in uw buik die aan de buitenkant te voelen is Uw huisarts of andere arts stuurt u door naar een specialist in nierziektes (uroloog) voor meer onderzoek. Om te onderzoeken of u nierkanker heeft, krijgt u een CT-scan van uw buik. Hiermee kan de uroloog zien of er een gezwel in uw nier zit en hoe groot deze is. Als het gezwel op uw nier kleiner is dan 4 centimeter, krijgt u vaak ook een röntgenfoto van uw borstkas . Hiermee kan de arts zien of u uitzaaiingen heeft van het gezwel. Als het gezwel groter is dan 4 centimeter, krijgt u vaak een CT-scan van uw borstkas. Hierop is nog beter te zien of het gezwel is uitgezaaid naar andere organen, zoals uw longen. De uroloog bespreekt met u wat u wilt en welke behandelingen bij u mogelijk zijn. Dit hangt af van bijvoorbeeld: hoe groot het gezwel in uw nier is of u nog andere ziektes heeft Als het gezwel kleiner is dan 4 centimeter, kan de arts eerst een klein stukje nier (biopt) weghalen om te onderzoeken in het laboratorium. In het laboratorium kunnen ze meestal zien of het gezwel kanker is. 3 van de 10 gezwellen kleiner dan 4 centimeter zijn geen kanker. Als de artsen denken dat het geen kanker is, is een behandeling niet altijd nodig. U krijgt dan regelmatig controles om te zien of het gezwel groeit. Later kunt u toch nog een behandeling krijgen, als dat nodig is. Soms kunnen ze in het laboratorium niet duidelijk zien of het kanker is. Uw arts bespreekt met u wat dan het beste bij uw situatie past. Bijvoorbeeld nog een stukje nier weghalen om te onderzoeken, regelmatig controles of een behandeling van het gezwel.

patient_question: I have a tumor in my kidney and am receiving treatment in which I keep my kidney
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• You have a tumor in your kidney.
• Sometimes you will only have checks to see if the tumor is growing.
• Treatment depends on how big the tumor is and where it is located.
• Treatments may include:
  ◦ removing part of the kidney through surgery
  ◦ heating or freezing the tumor with a needle
• An operation poses more risks.
• You and your doctor will discuss what treatment you want and can receive.
english_full: If the tumor is still very small, the doctor can first remove a small piece of kidney (biopsy) for examination in the laboratory. In the laboratory they can see whether the tumor is cancer. 3 out of 10 growths smaller than 4 centimeters are not cancer. If your doctor thinks it is not cancer, the doctor will discuss with you whether treatment is necessary. Or that you have regular check-ups to see if the tumor is growing. If necessary, you can still receive treatment later. The tumor in your kidney usually needs to be treated immediately. There are various treatments that allow you to keep your kidney or part of the kidney: An operation to remove part of the kidney. This can be done with open surgery, keyhole surgery or surgery with the help of an operating robot. During keyhole surgery, the doctor operates through small incisions in your abdomen. The doctor inserts thin tubes into your abdomen through these incisions. The doctor can insert a small camera and tools into your abdomen through the tubes, such as scissors and forceps. This allows the doctor to remove the tumor through the tubes. Keyhole surgery can also be done with a surgical robot. The doctor sits a few meters away from you and works with the robot. During open surgery, you will receive a large incision in your abdomen or side. The doctor removes part of your kidney through the incision. During keyhole surgery, the doctor operates through small incisions in your abdomen. The doctor inserts thin tubes into your abdomen through these incisions. The doctor can insert a small camera and tools into your abdomen through the tubes, such as scissors and forceps. This allows the doctor to remove the tumor through the tubes. Keyhole surgery can also be done with a surgical robot. The doctor sits a few meters away from you and works with the robot. During open surgery, you will receive a large incision in your abdomen or side. The doctor removes part of your kidney through the incision. A minor operation to heat or freeze the tumor in the kidney. The doctor inserts a needle into the tumor. This heats or freezes the tumor, causing it to break. Your body clears away the remains of the tumor itself. You and your doctor will discuss what treatment you want and can receive. The doctor will discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the treatments with you. And the doctor will discuss which treatment is possible for you. This depends, for example, on how old you are, how ill you are and whether you have any other illnesses. If the tumor in your kidney is larger than 7 centimeters, your entire kidney will usually be removed. Sometimes the large tumor is not cancer, but it is better to remove the tumor. If you only have checks to see if the growth is growing, you may not have any problems with surgery. You do not have a wound from an operation. Some people find this period of check-ups very difficult, because they do not know whether the tumor is growing between check-ups. If the tumor has become larger, you may receive further treatment. If part of your kidney has been removed during surgery, this kidney can usually continue to perform its functions well. Even if the entire kidney has been removed, you can still live well with the other kidney. Usually the other kidney works well enough that you do not need dialysis or a donor kidney.

patient_question: I have a tumor in my kidney and my kidney is being removed
canonical_question: Who can help me if I think my care is being delayed?
english_summary:
• The entire kidney is almost always removed if the tumor is larger than 7 centimeters.
• The entire kidney is also removed if the tumor is in an awkward location in the kidney.
• You will have open surgery (an incision in your abdomen) or keyhole surgery.
• You and your doctor will discuss which operation you can and want to have.
english_full: If the tumor in your kidney is larger than 7 centimeters, your entire kidney will usually be removed. The tumor is then too large to remove only part of the kidney. Even if the tumor is in a difficult spot, your entire kidney will usually be removed. For example, if it is near an artery. The doctor cannot then just remove part of the kidney. There are two types of operations available to remove the kidney: keyhole surgery: the doctor operates through small incisions in your abdomen. The doctor inserts thin tubes into your abdomen through these incisions. The doctor can insert a small camera and tools into your abdomen through the tubes, such as scissors and forceps. This allows the doctor to remove your kidney through the tubes. open surgery: you will have a large incision in your abdomen or side. The doctor removes your kidney through the incision. You and your doctor will discuss which operation you can and want to have. The doctor will discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the operations with you. And which operation is possible for you. This depends, for example, on how old you are, how ill you are and whether you have any other illnesses. Every operation has the potential for problems. For example: inflammation of the wound from the operation, bleeding at the site of the operation. After an open operation you are more likely to experience problems than after keyhole surgery. If the tumor is in 1 kidney, the doctor will only remove that kidney. You still keep your other kidney. 1 kidney is enough to continue to do the functions of the kidneys. Usually the other kidney works well enough that you do not need dialysis or a donor kidney. If your kidney and the tumor have been removed, there is a good chance that all the cancer cells have been removed. Once the kidney has been removed, it is examined in the laboratory. This way the doctor knows whether the entire tumor has been removed. The doctor also knows whether the tumor is cancerous or not. If the tumor is not cancerous, you do not need any further action or checks. If the tumor is cancerous, your doctor will discuss with you how to proceed. You usually do not need any further treatments. You will have regular check-ups to see if the tumor returns and if you develop any complaints.

patient_question: I have metastatic kidney cancer
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• Kidney cancer can spread to other organs.
• Treatments have advantages and disadvantages.
• They can reduce complaints and sometimes ensure that you live longer.
• You choose together with your doctor which treatment suits you best.
• Talk to your doctor, nurse, family and friends about what you find important.
english_full: Kidney cancer often does not cause any symptoms in the beginning. As a result, the disease is already serious in many people when it is discovered. The cancer in the kidney is already large, or has even spread to other organs. The information on Thuisarts is about the most common type of kidney cancer. Doctors call this type of cancer renal cell cancer. 8 out of 10 people with kidney cancer have this type. Kidney cancer can spread to the: A CT scan or MRI can show whether you have metastases and where they are. The complaints you have depend on where the metastases are located and how large the metastases are. For example, you may have one or more of these complaints: fast and difficult breathing feeling nauseous, vomiting not feeling like eating different feeling in your arms or legs (tingling, less feeling) When you hear that the kidney cancer has spread and you are no longer getting better, it is very difficult. The future is uncertain. You don't know how long you will live, what plans you can still carry out, how long you will remain fit enough to do what you want to do. The quality of life is often the most important. You discuss your wishes and options together with your doctor and nurse. You can opt for treatment with medicines. As a result, kidney cancer grows less quickly, you may live longer and have fewer complaints. You can also opt for a treatment that only reduces the complaints. Sometimes you can have an operation to remove the metastases or the metastases can be irradiated.

patient_question: I have cancer pain. What can I do myself?
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• Tell your doctor or nurse that you have pain.
• Take painkillers as agreed with your doctor or nurse.
• Take good care of yourself: try to exercise regularly, relax and get enough sleep.
• Do less in a day. Do things when you feel good.
• Talk about your pain. For example, with your partner, family or good friends.
english_full: Call your doctor or nurse immediately if you are in so much pain that you can no longer cope. Or have someone else call for you. Also call if you have pain and one or more of these complaints: You have a fever (38 degrees or higher). You feel very sick. Make an appointment with the doctor or nurse for one or more of these things: You have questions about your treatment. Your pain does not diminish. You are bothered too much by your complaints. You may have pain from the cancer itself. For example, if the cancer grows in your organs, nerves or bones. Or pushes against it. Pain can also be caused by treatment, such as surgery, chemotherapy or radiation. For example, cancer treatment can cause severe mouth infections. You may also be more bothered by pain due to other things. What you think, feel and do has a major influence on the pain. For example, if you feel sad, scared or lonely. Or if you are afraid that moving will hurt. You may then suffer more from the pain. Have you been in a lot of pain for a long time? Then your brain may have become more sensitive to pain. Look at pain that lasts longer (chronic pain). Can the cancer no longer be cured? Then you can also be very tired. This can cause you to experience more pain. Discuss with your doctor or nurse if you are in pain. Also tell us if the pain changes or gets worse. Together you will see whether there is a treatment that can help you. For example with painkillers. Together with your doctor or nurse, you choose which painkillers you will use. And make a plan for how you will take the medications:

patient_question: Squamous cell cancer
english_full: Squamous cell cancer is cancer of the top layer of the skin. It often looks like a bump with skin. There may be a wound in it. It sometimes bleeds and does not heal well. If your GP thinks you have this skin cancer, go to the skin doctor. This usually cuts the spot away. Squamous cell cancer almost never goes to other places in the body.

patient_question: I have squamous cell cancer
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• Squamous cell cancer is cancer of the top layer of the skin.
• It often looks like a bump with skin.
• There may be a wound in it. It sometimes bleeds and does not heal well.
• If your GP thinks you have this skin cancer, go to the skin doctor.
• This usually excises the area.
• Squamous cell cancer almost never goes to other places in the body.
english_full: Squamous cell cancer is cancer in the top layer of your skin. Without treatment, the cancer can grow deeper into the skin. And in muscle and bone. This skin cancer almost never spreads to other places in the body. For most people, the skin cancer is removed in time. Then it can no longer spread. This skin cancer is most common in people over the age of 60. And much more often in people with light skin than in people with dark skin. Men have it almost twice as often as women. At first, squamous cell cancer looks like a pink bump. The top becomes harder and skins appear on it. A wound may appear in the middle. This does not heal well and can bleed quickly. Scabs may form on the bump or on the edge. Those scabs can come off. Then it may start bleeding again. If the spot is not removed, it will slowly get bigger. The place can take all kinds of shapes. More and more crusts and skins appear. Squamous cell cancer is mainly found on the skin that has received a lot of sunlight for years, such as: The risk of squamous cell cancer is greater: if you have had sunlight on your skin for years if you have had a lot of radiation from tanning beds if your skin has often been burned by the sun in the past if you were in the sun a lot as a child if you have light skin, light eyes, freckles, light blonde or red hair, and burn easily in the sun

Deze huidkanker komt het meest voor bij mensen ouder dan 60 jaar. En veel vaker bij mensen met een lichte huid dan bij mensen met een donkere huid. Mannen hebben het bijna 2 keer zo vaak als vrouwen. patient_question: I have prostate cancer that is only in the prostate. Which treatment is suitable for me?
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• These treatments can cure prostate cancer that is only in the prostate:
  ◦ surgery to remove the prostate
  ◦ irradiation from within the prostate
  ◦ irradiation from outside through the skin
• Together with your doctor you discuss which treatment you can and want to receive.
• You can also have it checked regularly to see whether the prostate cancer has grown.
• You can also choose not to treat the cancer.
english_full: Radiation from within Radiation from outside Prostate cancer that is only in the prostate can be in one or more places in your prostate. The cancer has not grown through the wall of the prostate. And there are no metastases. In half of men, prostate cancer that is only in the prostate grows slowly. Together with your doctor, you can choose not to treat prostate cancer immediately. For example, if the chance is very small that the cancer will continue to grow. The chance is therefore small that you will develop complaints from the cancer. You can then choose to have regular check-ups. It is then checked whether the prostate cancer is growing or changing. The checks are done in the hospital by a doctor or nurse who knows a lot about prostate cancer. Is your prostate cancer dangerous? For example, do the cancer cells grow very quickly? Then you can choose one of these treatments with the aim of healing: surgery to remove the prostate and seminal vesicles radiation from inside the prostate radiation from outside through the skin You can also choose not to receive treatment or check-ups. Perhaps the disadvantages of surgery or radiation outweigh the advantages for you. For example, because there are many side effects. Or because of your old age. You may no longer be fit enough for surgery or radiation. You still have an occasional appointment with the doctor in the hospital. You will then discuss together how you are doing. Maybe you have complaints and you want to do something about them. Together you will look at what you can do to reduce complaints. Together with the doctor you discuss which treatment you can and want to receive. This depends, for example, on how quickly the cancer cells grow, whether your prostate is large or small

The cancer cells are not dangerous. They grow slowly or they don't grow at all. Your doctor will tell you if you have these 3 things. Then the chance of the cancer growing further is very small. patient_question: I have prostate cancer and am receiving radiation from within the prostate
canonical_question: Can I be dismissed from my job during cancer treatment in the Netherlands?
english_summary:
• When irradiated from within, you get radioactive pieces of iodine in your prostate.
• These pieces of iodine destroy the cancer cells in the prostate.
• The iodine pieces are placed in your prostate in the hospital.
• The pieces of iodine remain in your body for the rest of your life.
• You may have problems with your bladder. And it may be difficult to get an erect penis.
• The doctor can help you deal with the problems. Sometimes you can get medication for these problems.
english_full: Advice after radiation The doctor places radioactive pieces of iodine in your prostate close to the cancer. This happens in the hospital. The radioactive pieces emit radiation. This radiation destroys the cancer cells. The radioactive pieces are only in your prostate. This means that the radiation only reaches your prostate and not the rest of your body. This way, healthy cells and organs receive as little radiation as possible. Doctors also call this type of radiation therapy internal radiation or brachytherapy. After irradiation from within the prostate with radioactive pieces of iodine you have a good chance of recovery. After treatment, you will live as long as men who do not have prostate cancer. You will have a consultation with the doctor who will arrange the anesthesia beforehand. On the day itself you should have an empty stomach and empty intestines. The doctor will discuss with you which type of anesthesia you will receive: You will receive anesthesia with an epidural injection. This injection makes you numb from your navel to your legs and feet. This way you won't feel anything when the doctor places the pieces of radioactive iodine in your prostate. You are put to sleep with medication. This is called anesthesia. You will therefore not notice anything when the doctor places the pieces of radioactive iodine in your prostate. On the day that the iodine pieces are placed, it is important that your stomach and intestines are empty. That is why you usually have to eat and drink differently on the day of treatment. Sometimes you have to empty your intestines the day before with an enema or laxative. You will hear from the hospital which rules apply to you. For example, one of these rules: You drink a drink the night before that will empty your intestines quickly. This is called an enema. You are not allowed to eat anything from midnight until the treatment. You may drink clear drinks up to 2 hours before the treatment. Such as water, tea and soft drinks without sparkling.

Deze straling maakt de kankercellen kapot. De radioactieve stukjes zitten alleen in je prostaat. Daardoor komt de straling alleen in je prostaat en niet in de rest van je lichaam. Zo krijgen gezonde cellen en organen zo weinig mogelijk straling. Artsen noemen deze soort bestraling ook wel inwendige bestraling of brachytherapie. Na bestraling van binnenuit de prostaat met radioactieve stukjes jodium heb je een grote kans om te genezen. Na de behandeling leef je even lang als mannen die geen prostaatkanker hebben. Vooraf krijg je een gesprek met de arts die de verdoving regelt. Op de dag zelf moet je een lege maag en lege darmen hebben. De arts bespreekt met je welke soort verdoving je krijgt: Je krijgt verdoving met een ruggenprik. Door deze prik ben je verdoofd van je navel tot en met je benen en voeten. patient_question: I have prostate cancer and am receiving external radiation through the skin
canonical_question: Where can I get reliable advice on nutrition and diet during cancer treatment in the Netherlands?
english_summary:
• When irradiated from outside through the skin, radiation destroys the cancer cells in the prostate.
• This radiation can cause immediate side effects. For example, itching or red spots on the skin.
• You may also experience problems after radiation. For example with your intestines, bladder or penis.
• The doctor can help you deal with the problems and side effects.
english_full: With external radiation through the skin, the radiation comes from a device that rotates around your hips. As a result, radiation comes from different directions through your skin into the prostate. The radiation destroys all the cancer cells in the prostate. The radiation can also damage healthy cells near the prostate. That can cause side effects. Doctors also call this type of radiation therapy external beam radiation or radiotherapy. After external irradiation through the skin you have a good chance of healing. After radiation, you live as long as men who do not have prostate cancer. There are different types of devices that can deliver radiation from the outside through the skin. This depends on which hospital you are in. The preparation differs per type of radiation. You will always first receive a CT scan of your prostate. Sometimes you get pieces of gold or metal in your prostate before the radiation treatment. These pieces are clearly visible on the CT scan. This way the doctor knows exactly where the radiation should take place. Sometimes the doctor draws lines or dots on your skin. That is also to know where your prostate is. You will often also receive an MRI. The cancer and the prostate can then be seen even better. Radiation can begin approximately 1 or 2 weeks after the CT scan. With external radiation you lie on a kind of table. A device rotates around your hips. As a result, radiation comes from different directions through your skin and into the prostate. The radiation destroys all the cancer cells in the prostate. The radiation can also damage healthy cells near the prostate. That can cause side effects. You will receive radiation approximately 5 times a week. Each irradiation is short and lasts no longer than 5 minutes. In total you will receive 20 radiation treatments in about a month. If you have no problems urinating, the radiation treatment may last less than a month. Anesthesia is not necessary. After each radiation treatment you can go home straight away. External radiation through the skin can cause immediate side effects. You may also experience problems after radiation.

Soms tekent de arts lijnen of punten op je huid. Ook dat is om te weten waar je prostaat zit. Vaak krijg je ook een MRI . De kanker en de prostaat zijn dan nog beter te zien. Ongeveer 1 of 2 weken na de CT-scan kan de bestraling beginnen. Bij bestraling van buitenaf lig je op een soort tafel. Een apparaat draait om je heupen heen. Daardoor komt uit verschillende richtingen straling door je huid heen in de prostaat. De straling maakt alle kankercellen in de prostaat kapot. De straling kan ook gezonde cellen in de buurt van de prostaat beschadigen. Dat kan zorgen voor bijwerkingen. Je krijgt ongeveer 5 keer per week een bestraling. Iedere bestraling is kort en duurt niet langer dan 5 minuten. In totaal krijg je 20 keer een bestraling in ongeveer een maand. Als je geen problemen met plassen hebt, kan de bestraling korter dan een maand duren. Verdoving is niet nodig. Na elke bestraling mag je direct naar huis. Bestraling van buitenaf door de huid heen kan direct bijwerkingen geven. Ook kun je na de bestraling problemen krijgen.

patient_question: I have prostate cancer and am having surgery to remove my prostate
canonical_question: Which hospital should I go to for my type of cancer?
english_summary:
• During prostate cancer surgery, the doctor removes your prostate and seminal vesicles.
• This can be done with keyhole surgery or with open surgery.
• You may experience complaints after the operation. Like not being able to hold your pee properly anymore. Or no longer getting or keeping an erect penis.
• You can receive treatments for these complaints.
• You will have check-ups after the operation to see whether the prostate cancer has disappeared.
english_full: The doctor surgically removes the entire prostate and the seminal vesicles. There are 2 seminal vesicles behind the prostate. The prostate and seminal vesicles produce fluid. When you ejaculate, this fluid comes out with the sperm cells. The prostate is located around the urethra. The doctor also removes a piece of the urethra. Sometimes the doctor also removes lymph nodes near the prostate. The laboratory is examining whether it also contains cancer cells. The operation can be performed in two ways: via several small incisions in the abdomen (keyhole surgery) via 1 large incision in the abdomen (open surgery). Most people undergo keyhole surgery. After keyhole surgery you often get better faster and have less pain. An operation to remove the prostate and seminal vesicles is suitable for the following: You are not overweight. You have no other serious illnesses. 3 out of 4 men with prostate cancer whose prostate has been removed are still alive after 10 years. That is just as much as in men who do not have prostate cancer. Before the operation you will have an interview with the doctor who performs the operation. The doctor will tell you how the operation is going. You can also ask questions. The doctor will tell you, for example: whether lymph nodes will also be removed, whether you will have keyhole surgery or an operation through an incision in your abdomen. The doctor will also discuss with you the risk of problems after the operation. Like not being able to hold your pee properly. Or it is difficult or impossible to get an erect penis.
original_dutch: De arts haalt met een operatie de hele prostaat en de zaadblaasjes weg. Achter de prostaat zitten 2 zaadblaasjes. De prostaat en de zaadblaasjes maken vocht. Als je klaarkomt, komt dit vocht met de zaadcellen naar buiten. De prostaat ligt rondom de plasbuis. Daardoor haalt de arts ook een stukje van de plasbuis weg. Soms haalt de arts ook lymfeklieren weg in de buurt van de prostaat. In het laboratorium wordt onderzocht of daar ook kankercellen inzitten. De operatie kan op 2 manieren: via meer kleine sneetjes in de buik (kijkoperatie) via 1 grote snee in de buik (open operatie) De meeste mensen krijgen een kijkoperatie. Na een kijkoperatie ben je vaak sneller beter en heb je minder pijn. Een operatie om de prostaat en zaadblaasjes weg te halen is geschikt bij deze dingen: Je hebt geen overgewicht. Je hebt geen andere ernstige ziektes. 3 van de 4 mannen met prostaatkanker bij wie de prostaat is weggehaald, leven nog na 10 jaar. Dat is net zoveel als bij mannen die geen prostaatkanker hebben. Voor de operatie krijg je een gesprek met de arts die de operatie doet. Je hoort van de arts hoe de operatie gaat. Je kunt ook vragen stellen. De arts vertelt je bijvoorbeeld: of er ook lymfeklieren weggehaald worden of je een kijkoperatie krijgt of een operatie via een snee in je buik Ook bespreekt de arts met je hoe groot je kans is op problemen na de operatie. Zoals je plas niet goed kunnen ophouden. Of geen of moeilijk een stijve penis kunnen krijgen.

patient_question: I have metastatic prostate cancer. Which treatment is suitable for me?
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• You can no longer cure metastatic prostate cancer.
• There are treatments that ensure that the disease grows less quickly:
  ◦ anti-hormone treatment
  ◦ medications
  ◦ irradiation
  ◦ radioactive agent
• These treatments can help you live longer.
• These treatments can also reduce complaints. Like pain and being tired.
english_full: Anti-hormone treatment In metastatic prostate cancer, cancer cells have spread from your prostate to other parts of your body. These are metastases. For example, they can be in your bones or other organs. Or in the lymph nodes in your abdomen. You can no longer cure metastatic prostate cancer. There are treatments that ensure that the cancer grows less quickly. This allows you to live longer. When you hear that you have metastatic prostate cancer, it is usually very difficult. You may become uncertain about your future. Prostate cancer usually spreads to your bones. As a result, you may have the following complaints: weaker bones, which can easily break paralyzed legs This can happen if a metastasis in a vertebra presses on your spinal cord. You may also have other complaints with metastatic prostate cancer: pain in your abdomen or buttocks, feeling tired. This is more intense and different than just being tired. Urinary complaints because your prostate is larger or has been removed. For example, you have difficulty urinating. Or you have difficulty holding your urine. You can receive treatments against the cancer and your complaints: You can choose a treatment against prostate cancer. This makes the cancer grow less quickly. This means you will live longer and some complaints may diminish. You start with an anti-hormone treatment. These treatments cannot cure the cancer. They often have side effects. The side effects differ per treatment. You can also opt for treatments that reduce your complaints. Such as treatments for pain or urinary complaints. These treatments do not work against the cancer itself. They have fewer side effects than cancer treatments. These things are important when making a choice: Do you have many or few complaints due to prostate cancer?
original_dutch: Anti-hormoonbehandeling Bij uitgezaaide prostaatkanker zijn kankercellen vanuit je prostaat in andere delen van je lichaam gekomen. Dit zijn uitzaaiingen. Ze kunnen bijvoorbeeld in je botten of andere organen zitten. Of in de lymfeklieren in je buik. Je kunt niet meer genezen van uitgezaaide prostaatkanker. Er zijn wel behandelingen die zorgen dat de kanker minder snel groeit. Hierdoor kun je langer leven. Als je hoort dat je uitgezaaide prostaatkanker hebt, is dat meestal heel moeilijk. Je kunt onzeker worden over je toekomst. Prostaatkanker zaait meestal uit naar je botten. Hierdoor kun je deze klachten hebben: zwakkere botten, waardoor ze makkelijk kunnen breken verlamde benen Dit kan gebeuren als een uitzaaiing in een wervel drukt op je ruggenmerg. Ook kun je andere klachten hebben bij uitgezaaide prostaatkanker: pijn in je buik of billen moe zijn Dit is heftiger en anders dan gewoon moe zijn. plasklachten doordat je prostaat groter is of weggehaald is Je kunt bijvoorbeeld moeilijk plassen. Of je kunt je plas moeilijk ophouden. Je kunt behandelingen tegen de kanker en je klachten krijgen: Je kunt een behandeling kiezen tegen de prostaatkanker. Hierdoor groeit de kanker minder snel. Je blijft hierdoor langer leven en sommige klachten kunnen minder worden. Je begint met een anti-hormoonbehandeling. Deze behandelingen kunnen de kanker niet genezen. Ze hebben vaak bijwerkingen. De bijwerkingen verschillen per behandeling. Je kunt ook kiezen voor behandelingen die je klachten minder maken. Zoals behandelingen tegen pijn of plasklachten. Deze behandelingen werken niet tegen de kanker zelf. Ze hebben minder bijwerkingen dan behandelingen tegen de kanker. Om een keuze te maken zijn deze dingen belangrijk: Heb je veel of weinig klachten door prostaatkanker?

patient_question: Labia cancer
english_full: With labia cancer, a tumor of cancer cells grows in or around the labia. Treatment usually starts with surgery. You may also receive radiation, sometimes with chemotherapy. The treatment and the cancer itself can have consequences, for example problems with sex and psychological problems. Labia cancer can come back. That is why you often visit the doctor for a check-up after treatment. Pain at the entrance to the vagina. Smear from the cervix
original_dutch: Bij schaamlipkanker groeit er een gezwel van kankercellen in of rond de schaamlippen. De behandeling begint meestal met een operatie. U kunt ook bestraling krijgen, soms met chemotherapie. De behandeling en de kanker zelf kunnen gevolgen hebben, bijvoorbeeld problemen met seks en psychische problemen. Schaamlipkanker kan terugkomen. Daarom komt u na de behandeling vaak op controle bij de arts. Pijn bij ingang vagina Uitstrijkje baarmoederhals

patient_question: I have troubled cells that can become labia cancer due to HPV
canonical_question: How do I get referred to a cancer specialist?
english_summary:
• HPV is a virus. It can be in different places.
• Sometimes HPV causes troubled or changed cells in the skin of the labia or at the entrance to the vagina.
• Labia cancer can develop from these cells.
• There are 3 treatments:
  ◦ a cream
  ◦ treatment with a laser
  ◦ a minor operation
• After the operation, restless cells may appear again. That is why you remain under the supervision of the gynecologist.
english_full: Treatment with cream Treatment with laser HPV is a virus. The virus is on or in the skin and mucous membranes. For example, mucous membranes are located near the labia and in the vagina, cervix, penis, mouth and throat. You can get the virus through sex. Almost everyone gets infected once or more. The immune system usually kills the virus. But sometimes that doesn't work. Then cells in the skin or mucous membranes can change or become restless. Cancer can later develop from these cells. If the virus is near your labia or at the entrance to the vagina, troubled cells can form there. These can later become labia cancer. The chance of that happening is small. Of every 100 women with troubled cells, about 3 will develop labia cancer. When this happens, it is usually in women between the ages of 30 and 40. If you have troubled cells due to HPV that can become labia cancer, you may suffer from: dark or light spots on the labia, thicker spots in the skin of the labia, a kind of warts. These resemble genital warts. But the treatment for genital warts does not help. itching, pain or burning sensation in the labia If the doctor thinks that you have troubled cells, he will refer you to a gynecologist in the hospital. The gynecologist will examine your labia, the entrance to the vagina and the area around it. The doctor can also look inside the vagina to see what the inside of the vagina and the cervix look like. You discuss with the gynecologist which treatment best suits your situation. That depends, for example, on how large the area with restless cells is. There are 3 treatments: a cream with imiquimod a treatment with a laser Sometimes a combination of these treatments is necessary.
original_dutch: Behandeling met crème Behandeling met laser HPV is een virus. Het virus zit op of in de huid en in de slijmvliezen. Slijmvliezen zitten bijvoorbeeld bij de schaamlippen en in de vagina, baarmoedermond, penis, mond en keel. U kunt het virus krijgen via seks. Bijna iedereen raakt wel 1 keer of vaker besmet. Meestal maakt de afweer het virus dood. Maar soms lukt dat niet. Dan kunnen cellen in de huid of slijmvliezen veranderen of onrustig worden. Uit die cellen kan later kanker ontstaan. Als het virus bij uw schaamlippen of bij de ingang van de vagina zit, kunnen daar onrustige cellen komen. Die kunnen later schaamlipkanker worden. De kans dat dat gebeurt, is klein. Van de 100 vrouwen met onrustige cellen krijgen er ongeveer 3 schaamlipkanker. Als dit gebeurt, is dat meestal bij vrouwen tussen de 30 en 40 jaar. Als u door HPV onrustige cellen heeft die schaamlipkanker kunnen worden, kunt u last hebben van: donkere of lichte plekjes op de schaamlippen dikkere plekken in de huid van de schaamlippen een soort wratjes Deze lijken op genitale wratten . Maar de behandeling voor genitale wratten helpt niet. jeuk, pijn of een branderig gevoel bij de schaamlippen Als de huisarts denkt dat u onrustige cellen heeft, stuurt die u door naar een gynaecoloog in het ziekenhuis. De gynaecoloog bekijkt uw schaamlippen, de ingang van de vagina en het gebied eromheen. Ook kan de arts in de vagina kijken om te zien hoe de binnenkant van de vagina en de baarmoederhals er uitzien. U bespreekt met de gynaecoloog welke behandeling het beste bij uw situatie past. Dat hangt bijvoorbeeld af van hoe groot de plek met onrustige cellen is. Er zijn 3 behandelingen: een crème met imiquimod een behandeling met een laser Soms is een combinatie van deze behandelingen nodig.

patient_question: I have labia cancer
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• In labia cancer, a tumor of cancer cells grows in or around the labia.
• Treatment usually begins with surgery.
• You may also receive radiation, sometimes with chemotherapy.
• The treatment and the cancer itself can have consequences, for example problems with sex and psychological problems.
• Labia cancer can come back.
• That is why you often visit the doctor for a check-up after treatment.
english_full: With labia cancer, a tumor of cancer cells grows in the labia. This is called a tumor. The tumor can be on the outer or inner labia. The tumor can also be located near the clitoris, at the entrance to the vagina or between the labia and the anus (poo hole). Cells may become detached from the tumor. They can come from somewhere else in the body. These are metastases. They usually occur in the lymph nodes in the groin. Very occasionally the cells end up in other organs, such as the lungs or liver. There are different types of labia cancer. Labia cancer usually arises in the skin cells of the labia. This is called squamous cell carcinoma. This information is only about squamous cell carcinoma. Most women with labia cancer are over 60 years old. With labia cancer you may experience complaints in your labia and entrance to your vagina (vulva): pain or a burning sensation when urinating, discharge that is different than normal, a wound that does not heal. These complaints can also be caused by something else. For example, a fungus, an STD or a bladder infection. It is not clear what causes labia cancer. However, some women have a greater chance of developing labia cancer. This concerns: women with the skin disease lichen sclerosus. If this skin disease is located near the labia and around the entrance to the vagina, a pre-cancerous stage can develop. This means that there are changed cells that can become cancerous. Of 100 women with lichen sclerosus, 5 will develop labia cancer. women who have troubled cells due to HPV HPV is a virus (human papillomavirus). HPV is passed on through sex. Almost everyone who has had sex has been infected with HPV. Your immune system usually kills the virus. Sometimes that doesn't work. A pre-stage of labia cancer can then develop. This means that there are changed cells. If you have heard that you have labia cancer, you will have tests to see how extensive your labia cancer is. The doctor can use research to determine how large the tumor is
original_dutch: Bij schaamlipkanker groeit er een gezwel van kankercellen in de schaamlippen. Dit heet een tumor. De tumor kan op de buitenste of de binnenste schaamlippen zitten. De tumor kan ook zitten bij de clitoris, bij de ingang van de vagina of tussen de schaamlippen en de anus (poepgat). Er kunnen cellen van de tumor losraken. Die kunnen ergens anders in het lichaam komen. Dit zijn uitzaaiingen. Meestal komen ze in de lymfeklieren in de liezen. Heel soms komen de cellen in andere organen, zoals de longen of lever. Er zijn verschillende soorten schaamlipkanker. Meestal ontstaat schaamlipkanker in de huidcellen van de schaamlippen. Dit heet plaveiselcel-carcinoom. Deze informatie gaat alleen over plaveiselcel-carcinoom. De meeste vrouwen met schaamlipkanker zijn ouder dan 60 jaar. Bij schaamlipkanker kunt u klachten krijgen bij uw schaamlippen en ingang van uw vagina (vulva): pijn of een branderig gevoel bij plassen afscheiding die anders is dan normaal een wondje dat niet geneest Deze klachten kunnen ook door iets anders komen. Bijvoorbeeld een schimmel, een soa of een blaasontsteking. Het is niet duidelijk waardoor schaamlipkanker ontstaat. Wel hebben sommige vrouwen een grotere kans om schaamlipkanker te krijgen. Het gaat om: vrouwen met de huidziekte lichen sclerosus Als deze huidziekte bij de schaamlippen en rond de ingang van de vagina zit, kan een voorstadium van kanker ontstaan. Dit betekent dat er veranderde cellen zijn die kanker kunnen worden. Van de 100 vrouwen met lichen sclerosus krijgen er 5 schaamlipkanker. vrouwen die onrustige cellen hebben door HPV HPV is een virus (humaan papillomavirus). HPV wordt doorgegeven door seks. Bijna iedereen die seks heeft (gehad), is wel eens besmet geweest met HPV. Meestal maakt uw afweer het virus dood. Soms lukt dat niet. Dan kan een voorstadium van schaamlipkanker ontstaan. Dit betekent dat er veranderde cellen zijn. Als u heeft gehoord dat u schaamlipkanker heeft, krijgt u onderzoeken om te kijken hoe uitgebreid de schaamlipkanker bij u is. De arts kan met onderzoek bepalen: hoe groot de tumor is

patient_question: I have labia cancer and am having surgery
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• Treatment usually begins with surgery.
• Even if you can no longer recover, you can have an operation.
• The gynecologist removes the tumor and a piece of tissue around it.
• The doctor often also removes 1 or more lymph nodes in your groin.
• The tumor and lymph nodes are examined.
• You will receive the results after 5 to 10 days. You will then hear whether further treatments are required.
• It may take a few weeks for the wounds to heal.
• After the operation, surgery or radiation is sometimes necessary.
english_full: Lymph node surgery The treatment of labia cancer usually starts with surgery. The purpose of the surgery is to cure you of cancer. Can you no longer be cured of cancer? Then you can also undergo surgery. For example, if you suffer a lot from the tumor. You will stay in the hospital for a few days after the operation. Before the operation you will have a consultation with the doctor who performs the operation. Or with a nurse specialist. The conversation is about: what the advantages and disadvantages of those choices are how much the doctor has to remove: for example part of the labia or the entire labia, or the clitoris the risks and consequences of the operation how long it takes before you recover what you can and cannot do after the operation You will also have a conversation with the doctor who arranges the anesthesia (anesthesiologist). This will monitor your breathing and heart rate during the operation. This doctor needs a lot of information from you. For example, whether you have other diseases and what medications you take. The doctor will also discuss how the anesthesia works and how you prepare for the operation, for example: whether you can eat and drink before the operation or whether you should stop taking your medications, such as blood thinners. For tips on a good conversation with your doctors, see joint decision-making with my doctor. Would you like to know more about the preparation? Also read preparing for surgery.
original_dutch: Operatie lymfeklieren De behandeling van schaamlipkanker begint meestal met een operatie. Het doel van de operatie is om u te genezen van kanker. Kunt u niet meer genezen van kanker? Dan kunt u ook geopereerd worden. Bijvoorbeeld als u veel last heeft van de tumor. U blijft na de operatie een paar dagen in het ziekenhuis. Voor de operatie heeft u een gesprek met de arts die de operatie doet. Of met een verpleegkundig specialist. Het gesprek gaat over: wat de voordelen en nadelen van die keuzes zijn hoeveel de arts moet weghalen: bijvoorbeeld een deel van de schaamlip of de hele schaamlip, of de clitoris de risico’s en gevolgen van de operatie hoe lang het duurt voordat u hersteld bent wat u na de operatie wel en niet kunt doen U krijgt ook een gesprek met de arts die de verdoving regelt (anesthesioloog). Deze let tijdens de operatie op uw ademhaling en hartslag. Deze arts heeft veel informatie van u nodig. Bijvoorbeeld of u andere ziekten heeft en welke medicijnen u gebruikt. Verder bespreekt de arts hoe de verdoving gaat en hoe u zich voorbereidt op de operatie, bijvoorbeeld: of u mag eten en drinken voor de operatie of u moet stoppen met uw medicijnen, zoals bloedverdunners Kijk voor tips voor een goed gesprek met uw artsen bij samen beslissen met mijn arts . Wilt u meer weten over de voorbereiding? Lees dan ook voorbereiden op een operatie .

patient_question: Esophageal cancer
english_full: For example, with esophageal cancer you have pain when swallowing. Or your food doesn't turn out well. You will usually have more tests, such as a CT scan. You can get chemotherapy and radiation to get better. Sometimes you will then have surgery to remove the cancer. If you can no longer get better, you can receive treatment to ensure that the cancer grows less quickly. Or against your complaints. Being tired because of cancer that can no longer be cured
original_dutch: Bij slokdarmkanker heb je bijvoorbeeld pijn bij het slikken. Of je eten zakt niet goed. Je krijgt meestal meer onderzoeken, zoals een CT-scan. Je kunt chemotherapie met bestraling krijgen om beter te worden. Soms krijg je daarna een operatie om de kanker weg te halen. patient_question: I have esophageal cancer and am getting more tests
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• You have had a visual examination of your esophagus.
• If it turns out that you have esophageal cancer, you will usually receive more tests.
• This allows the doctor to see how big the cancer is. And whether the cancer has grown or spread to other parts of your body.
• You discuss with the doctor which treatments you can and want to receive.
english_full: Esophageal cancer is cancer in the esophagus. The esophagus is a kind of tube between your throat and your stomach. When you swallow food, it goes through your esophagus to your stomach. These two types of esophageal cancer are the most common: Adenocarcinoma. This type of cancer is almost always in the lower part of the esophagus. For example, you can get it if you have often suffered from heartburn for years. Squamous cell carcinoma. This is usually in the upper or middle part of the esophagus. But it can also be at the bottom of the esophagus. The cancer can grow through the outside of the esophagus. Sometimes the cancer continues to grow into the trachea or aorta, for example. That is the largest artery in your body. The cancer can also spread. Cancer cells then move from your esophagus via your blood to another place in the body. For example, your liver or your lungs. Esophageal cancer can also spread through the lymph nodes. These are a kind of small spheres that are located in many places in your body. They are important for your immune system. The cancer can spread from the lymph nodes around your esophagus to other lymph nodes in your body. You can notice this with esophageal cancer: Your poop is black. This could be due to blood in your poop. You lose weight without doing anything for it. Your food gets stuck or doesn't sink properly. You have pain or a feeling of fullness in the center of your chest, behind your breastbone. You have pain when swallowing. You feel less like eating. You cough and choke during or after eating.
original_dutch: Bij slokdarmkanker zit er kanker in de slokdarm. De slokdarm is een soort buis tussen je keel en je maag. Als je eten doorslikt, gaat het via je slokdarm naar je maag. Deze twee soorten slokdarmkanker komen het meest voor: Het adeno-carcinoom. Deze soort kanker zit bijna altijd in het onderste deel van de slokdarm. Je kunt het bijvoorbeeld krijgen als je jarenlang vaak last hebt van brandend maagzuur . Het plaveiselcel-carcinoom. Dat zit meestal in het bovenste of middelste deel van de slokdarm. Maar het kan ook onderin de slokdarm zitten. De kanker kan door de buitenkant van de slokdarm heen groeien. Soms groeit de kanker dan bijvoorbeeld door in de luchtpijp of in de aorta. Dat is de grootste slagader in je lichaam. De kanker kan ook uitzaaien. Er gaan dan kankercellen uit je slokdarm via je bloed naar een andere plek in het lichaam. Bijvoorbeeld je lever of je longen. Slokdarmkanker kan ook uitzaaien via de lymfeklieren. Dit zijn een soort kleine bolletjes die op veel plekken in je lichaam zitten. Ze zijn belangrijk voor je afweer. De kanker kan van de lymfeklieren rondom je slokdarm naar andere lymfeklieren in je lichaam gaan. Dit kun je merken bij slokdarmkanker: Je poep is zwart. Dit kan komen door bloed in je poep. Je valt af zonder dat je daar iets voor doet. Je eten blijft steken of zakt niet goed. Je hebt pijn of een vol gevoel in het midden van je borst, achter je borstbeen. Je hebt pijn bij het slikken. Je hebt minder zin om te eten. Je hoest en verslikt je tijdens of na het eten.

patient_question: I have esophageal cancer and can receive treatment to get better
canonical_question: Where can I get reliable advice on nutrition and diet during cancer treatment in the Netherlands?
english_summary:
• If you can get better from esophageal cancer, you can get these treatments:
  ◦ chemotherapy, radiation and then surgery
  ◦ only chemotherapy with radiation
• Make sure you are as fit as possible before the treatment.
english_full: Chemotherapy, radiation and surgery Chemotherapy with radiation You can receive treatment to get better if all of these things are the case for you: The cancer has not grown into the organs near the esophagus. No cancer cells (metastases) can be seen in the lymph nodes that are further from the esophagus. Lymph nodes are like small spheres that are located in many places in your body. They are important for your immune system. The cancer has not spread to other organs. You are fit enough for the treatment. If you can get better from esophageal cancer, you can receive these treatments: chemotherapy, radiation and then surgery only chemotherapy with radiation. Very occasionally, the doctor can remove the cancer during an examination. Together with your doctor, you can determine which treatment best suits your situation. You discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the treatment. And what you can expect. You can always say no to treatment. What you want is important. Are you unsure about a treatment? Then discuss this with your doctor and the people you love. You often first receive chemotherapy with radiation. Chemotherapy is a treatment with medicines. The drugs can destroy cancer cells. Or they ensure that the cancer cells grow less. During radiation, as many cancer cells as possible are destroyed with X-rays. Chemotherapy makes radiation work better. Chemotherapy with radiation usually makes the cancer smaller. The doctor can then better remove the cancer with surgery. Chemotherapy with radiation can also reduce the chance that the cancer will come back. After chemotherapy and radiation, you will have an operation. The doctor removes the cancer.
original_dutch: Chemotherapie, bestraling en operatie Chemotherapie met bestraling Je kunt een behandeling krijgen om beter te worden als al deze dingen bij jou zo zijn: De kanker is niet doorgegroeid in de organen die in de buurt van de slokdarm liggen. Er zijn geen kankercellen (uitzaaiingen) te zien in de lymfeklieren die verder van de slokdarm liggen. Lymfeklieren zijn een soort kleine bolletjes die op veel plekken in je lichaam zitten. Ze zijn belangrijk voor je afweer. De kanker is niet uitgezaaid naar andere organen. Je bent fit genoeg voor de behandeling. Als je beter kunt worden van slokdarmkanker, kun je deze behandelingen krijgen: chemotherapie, bestraling en daarna een operatie alleen chemotherapie met bestraling Heel soms kan de arts de kanker weghalen tijdens een kijk-onderzoek. Met je arts bekijk je welke behandeling het beste past bij jouw situatie. Jullie bespreken de voordelen en nadelen van de behandeling. En wat je kunt verwachten. Je kunt altijd nee zeggen tegen een behandeling. Wat je zelf wilt, is belangrijk. Twijfel je over een behandeling? Bespreek dit dan met je arts en de mensen van wie je houdt. Je krijgt vaak eerst chemotherapie met bestraling . Chemotherapie is een behandeling met medicijnen. De medicijnen kunnen kankercellen kapotmaken. Of ze zorgen ervoor dat de kankercellen minder groeien. Bij bestraling worden zoveel mogelijk kankercellen kapotgemaakt met röntgenstralen. Door de chemotherapie werkt de bestraling beter. De chemotherapie met bestraling zorgt er meestal voor dat de kanker kleiner wordt. De arts kan de kanker dan beter weghalen met een operatie. Ook kan de chemotherapie met bestraling de kans kleiner maken dat de kanker terugkomt. Na de chemotherapie en bestraling krijg je een operatie . Daarbij haalt de arts de kanker weg.

patient_question: I have esophageal cancer and am receiving chemotherapy with radiation
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• Chemotherapy with radiation causes the cancer cells to break down and grow less.
• To get better, you can have chemotherapy with radiation and then surgery.
• Not everyone gets surgery. You also have a chance of getting better with chemotherapy and radiation alone.
english_full: You will receive chemotherapy and radiation at the same time. Chemotherapy is a treatment with medicines. These drugs can destroy cancer cells. Or they ensure that the cancer cells grow less. Irradiation is a treatment with X-rays. These rays can also destroy cancer cells. Chemotherapy makes radiation even more effective against the cancer. Have you agreed with your doctor that you will have surgery to remove the cancer? You will first receive chemotherapy with radiation to shrink the cancer. The doctor can usually remove the cancer better during the operation. Chemotherapy with radiation can also reduce the chance that the cancer will come back after surgery. You can also get chemotherapy and radiation alone to get better, without surgery. Not everyone can or wants to have surgery. For example, because the cancer is too high in the esophagus. Or because the operation poses too many risks. You will have an interview with a doctor who knows a lot about cancer (internist oncologist). The doctor asks you questions and you can also ask the doctor questions. The doctor will also discuss which medications you will receive, how often you will receive chemotherapy and what you can expect. Sometimes the doctor still conducts research. You will have an interview with the doctor who will irradiate you (radiation therapist). The doctor explains the treatment. He will also make a CT scan. This is to see where the cancer is and where the radiation should be delivered. The doctor places small dots of ink on your skin in these places. This way he can find them exactly. After the CT scan, the doctor will know how and how often you should receive radiation. It helps if you are as fit as possible before the treatment starts. Then you have a smaller chance of problems during and after the treatment. This is what you can do to become fitter: Quit smoking. If you smoke, you are more likely to have problems after surgery. Such as bleeding or blood clots (thrombosis). Exercise enough. A physiotherapist can help you with this. Make sure you don't lose any further weight. A dietitian can help you with this. If eating becomes increasingly difficult, you can be given nutritional supplements. You can also receive food and drink through a tube in your stomach (tube) or into a blood vessel (infusion). It is also important to have your teeth checked by the dentist. Chemotherapy can cause pain and sores in your mouth. It is therefore important that your mouth and teeth are healthy before you start chemotherapy. Take good care of your mouth. With chemotherapy, you receive medication through a tube into a blood vessel (infusion). You will usually receive chemotherapy in the outpatient clinic or day treatment. You will need to stay in the hospital for a few hours for this. Irradiation is a treatment with X-rays. You're under a radiation machine. The rays are focused very precisely on the cancer. However, the radiation can also damage healthy cells near the esophagus. You can get side effects from that.
original_dutch: Je krijgt tegelijk chemotherapie en bestraling. Chemotherapie is een behandeling met medicijnen. Die medicijnen kunnen kankercellen kapotmaken. Of ze zorgen ervoor dat de kankercellen minder groeien. Bestraling is een behandeling met röntgenstralen. Die stralen kunnen de kankercellen ook kapotmaken. Door de chemotherapie helpt de bestraling nog beter tegen de kanker. Heb je met je arts afgesproken dat je een operatie krijgt om de kanker weg te halen? Je krijgt dan eerst chemotherapie met bestraling om de kanker kleiner te maken. Meestal kan de arts de kanker dan beter weghalen tijdens de operatie. Ook kan de chemotherapie met bestraling de kans kleiner maken dat de kanker na de operatie terugkomt. Je kunt ook alleen chemotherapie met bestraling krijgen om beter te worden, zonder operatie. Niet iedereen kan of wil geopereerd worden. Bijvoorbeeld omdat de kanker te hoog in de slokdarm zit. Of omdat de operatie te veel risico's geeft. Je krijgt een gesprek met een arts die veel weet over kanker (internist-oncoloog). De arts stelt je vragen en jij kunt de arts ook vragen stellen. Ook bespreekt de arts welke medicijnen je krijgt, hoe vaak je chemotherapie krijgt en wat je kunt verwachten. Soms doet de arts nog onderzoek. Je krijgt een gesprek met de arts die je gaat bestralen (radiotherapeut). De arts legt de behandeling uit. Ook maakt die een CT-scan. Dit is om te zien waar de kanker zit en waar de bestraling moet komen. Op deze plekken zet de arts kleine puntjes met inkt op je huid. Zo kan die ze precies terugvinden. Na de CT-scan weet de arts hoe en hoe vaak je bestraald moet worden. Het helpt als je zo fit mogelijk bent voordat de behandeling begint. Dan heb je een kleinere kans op problemen tijdens en na de behandeling. Dit kun je doen om fitter te worden: Stop met roken . Als je rookt, heb je een grotere kans op problemen na de operatie. Zoals een bloeding of bloedproppen (trombose). Beweeg genoeg . Een fysiotherapeut kan je hierbij helpen. Zorg dat je niet verder afvalt. Een diëtist kan je hierbij helpen. Gaat eten steeds moeilijker, dan kun je drinkvoeding krijgen. Je kunt ook eten en drinken krijgen via een slangetje in je maag (sonde) of in een bloedvat (infuus). Het is ook belangrijk om je gebit te laten controleren door de tandarts. Door chemotherapie kun je pijn en wondjes in je mond krijgen. Het is daarom belangrijk dat je mond en gebit gezond zijn voordat je aan de chemotherapie begint. Verzorg je mond goed . Bij chemotherapie krijg je medicijnen via een slangetje in een bloedvat (infuus). Meestal krijg je de chemotherapie op de polikliniek of de dagbehandeling. Je moet hiervoor een paar uur in het ziekenhuis blijven. Bestraling is een behandeling met röntgenstralen. Je ligt onder een stralings-apparaat. De stralen worden heel precies op de kanker gericht. Toch kan de straling ook gezonde cellen in de buurt van de slokdarm beschadigen. Daar kun je bijwerkingen van krijgen.

patient_question: I have esophageal cancer and am having surgery
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• The purpose of the operation is to remove the esophageal cancer.
• The doctor removes almost the entire esophagus. And the upper part of the stomach.
• The doctor makes a new esophagus from the remaining part of the stomach.
• You will usually receive chemotherapy and radiation before the operation.
• You will need to eat, drink and sleep differently after the operation.
english_full: The first days after the operation An operation for esophageal cancer is a major operation. The goal is to remove the esophageal cancer. To do this, the doctor must remove almost the entire esophagus and the upper part of the stomach. The doctor also removes the lymph nodes around the esophagus. Lymph nodes are a kind of spheres that are located in many places in your body. They are important for your immune system. This can also contain cancer. The doctor makes a new esophagus from the piece of stomach that remains. This is called a tube stomach. If you have esophageal cancer and have surgery, it won't be right away. First you'll get these things: You'll usually get chemotherapy with radiation before the surgery. This takes about 5 weeks. You will have a few weeks of rest before the operation. After chemotherapy with radiation, you will receive a CT scan or a PET-CT scan. The doctor can then see whether the chemotherapy has worked. And whether cancer cells have ended up elsewhere in the body. We call that metastases. If metastases are visible on the scan, the operation cannot proceed. Then you can no longer get better. Before the operation you will have an interview with the doctor who operates on you. You discuss these things with the doctor: How is the operation going? What are the risks of the operation? What can you expect after the operation? If you have any questions about the operation, you can ask the doctor. Before the operation you will be put to sleep with medication. This is called anesthesia. This means you won't notice anything during the operation. Shortly before the day of the operation, you will have a consultation with the doctor who will put you to sleep (anesthesiologist). The doctor discusses how the anesthesia works. Do you have any questions about anesthesia or painkillers after the operation? Then you can ask them during this conversation. The doctor also examines you and asks you questions. For example:

Lymfeklieren zijn een soort bolletjes die op veel plekken in je lichaam zitten. Ze zijn belangrijk voor je afweer. Hier kan ook kanker in zitten. Van het stuk maag dat overblijft, maakt de arts een nieuwe slokdarm. Dit heet een buismaag. Als je slokdarmkanker hebt en een operatie krijgt, is dat niet meteen. Eerst krijg je deze dingen: Meestal krijg je voor de operatie chemotherapie met bestraling . Dit duurt ongeveer 5 weken. Voor de operatie heb je een aantal weken rust. Na de chemotherapie met bestraling krijg je een CT-scan of een PET-CT-scan . Daarop kan de arts zien of de chemotherapie heeft gewerkt. En of er geen kankercellen op een andere plek in het lichaam terecht zijn gekomen. We noemen dat uitzaaiingen. Als er uitzaaiingen te zien zijn op de scan, kan de operatie niet doorgaan. Je kunt dan niet meer beter worden . Voor de operatie krijg je een gesprek met de arts die je opereert . Met de arts bespreek je deze dingen: Hoe gaat de operatie? Wat zijn de risico's van de operatie? Wat kun je na de operatie verwachten? Als je nog vragen over de operatie hebt, kun je ze aan de arts stellen. Voor de operatie word je met medicijnen in slaap gebracht. Dit heet narcose. Je merkt daardoor niets van de operatie. Kort voor de dag van de operatie krijg je een gesprek met de arts die je in slaap brengt (anesthesioloog). De arts bespreekt hoe de narcose werkt. Heb je vragen over de narcose of over pijnstillers na de operatie? Dan kun je ze tijdens dit gesprek stellen. De arts onderzoekt je ook en stelt je vragen. Bijvoorbeeld:

patient_question: I have esophageal cancer and cannot get better
canonical_question: Where can I get reliable advice on nutrition and diet during cancer treatment in the Netherlands?
english_summary:
• Talk about what you find important in the last phase of your life. For example, with your doctor and the people you love.
• There are treatments to ensure that the cancer grows less quickly. Or that reduce your complaints.
• Together with your doctor, you look at which treatments you can receive and what you want.
english_full: Tube in the esophagus It is very difficult to hear that you cannot get better. The future is uncertain. You don't know how long you can keep doing the things you want to do. Talk about what you find important in your final phase of life. For example, with your family, friends and with the doctor. It gives you peace of mind when the people around you and your doctor know what you want. They can then help you with the choices you need to make. They can also give you the care that suits you. Thinking and talking about dying is not easy for most people. It is often difficult for yourself and the people around you to start talking about it. The decision aid about your wishes for care and treatment can help you. It can also be nice to talk to other people with esophageal cancer. This can be done, for example, through the Foundation for Patients with Digestive Cancer (SPKS). Has the cancer grown or gone to other parts of your body? Then you may experience these complaints, for example: You have difficulty swallowing. Your food no longer sinks properly into the esophagus. You have pain in the center of your chest, behind your breastbone. You have less appetite for food. You have pain in your stomach. Your skin or whites of your eyes are yellow. Your belly gets bigger. You breathe quickly or have difficulty breathing. You may also have psychological complaints. Such as being afraid, gloomy or sad. Or you may have trouble sleeping.
original_dutch: Buisje in de slokdarm Het is erg moeilijk om te horen dat je niet meer beter kunt worden. De toekomst is onzeker. Je weet niet hoe lang je de dingen kunt blijven doen die je wilt doen. Praat over wat jij belangrijk vindt in je laatste levensfase . Bijvoorbeeld met je familie, vrienden en met de arts. Het geeft rust als de mensen om je heen en je arts weten wat jij wilt. Zij kunnen je dan helpen bij de keuzes die je moet maken. Ook kunnen ze je de zorg geven die bij jou past. Denken en praten over doodgaan is voor de meeste mensen niet makkelijk. Voor jezelf en de mensen om je heen is het vaak moeilijk om erover te beginnen. De keuzehulp over je wensen voor zorg en behandeling kan je helpen. Het kan ook fijn zijn om te praten met andere mensen met slokdarmkanker. Dit kan bijvoorbeeld via de Stichting voor Patiënten met Kanker aan het Spijsverteringskanaal (SPKS) . Is de kanker gegroeid of naar andere delen van je lichaam gegaan? Dan kun je bijvoorbeeld deze klachten krijgen: Je hebt moeite met slikken. Je eten zakt niet meer goed in de slokdarm. Je hebt pijn in het midden van je borst, achter je borstbeen. Je hebt minder trek in eten. Je hebt pijn in je buik. Je huid of oogwit is geel. Je buik wordt dikker. Je haalt snel adem of ademhalen gaat moeilijk. Je kunt ook psychische klachten hebben. Zoals bang, somber of verdrietig zijn. Of je kunt slaapproblemen hebben.

patient_question: I have had treatment for esophageal cancer and still have complaints
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• After treatment for esophageal cancer you may continue to have complaints.
• Discuss your complaints with your GP or the doctor at the hospital.
• Often something can be done about it or you can learn to deal with it.
• There are many places where you can get help to get fitter again.
• It can help to talk to other people who have been treated for esophageal cancer.
english_full: Have you had treatment for esophageal cancer to get better? Then you may suffer from chemotherapy, radiation or surgery. You may have these complaints: You probably have to get used to the tube stomach. Eating no longer comes naturally. You may suffer from these things: stomach acid rising, less hunger and a quick feeling of fullness, suddenly not feeling well after eating (this is called dumping), vomiting or belching after eating, difficulty sleeping because you have to sleep half-sitting, difficulty swallowing or the feeling that the food is not going down properly. You can also suffer from this if you have not had surgery, but have only had chemotherapy and radiation. Pain. Eating may hurt after the treatment. Sometimes the pain has no clear cause. Being tired. You may be very tired after the treatment. This can take a very long time. Resting usually doesn't help. Sometimes there is a physical cause. Pain and psychological complaints can also make you very tired. Even after the treatment, your mind is often occupied with your illness. You may be afraid that the cancer will come back. Or you are gloomy or sad. This may also be because you have to learn to live with a tube stomach. After the operation it often takes months or years before you are fitter again. You can get help with this in many places. A dietitian can give you tips on how and what to eat with a tube stomach. You can be given temporary medication for some complaints caused by a tube stomach. For example against heartburn and diarrhea. Sometimes your food doesn't go down well. This may be because the place where the esophagus is attached to the stomach is too narrow. Or from scars from radiation, if you have not had surgery. The doctor can carefully widen such spots during a visual examination. It is important that you discuss your complaints with your GP or the doctor at the hospital. The doctor can investigate what causes your complaints. Sometimes there is a solution. Or are you getting better at dealing with it?
original_dutch: Heb je een behandeling voor slokdarmkanker gehad om beter te worden? Dan kun je last krijgen van de chemotherapie, de bestraling of de operatie. Deze klachten kun je hebben: Je moet waarschijnlijk erg wennen aan de buismaag. Eten gaat niet meer vanzelf. Je kunt last hebben van deze dingen: maagzuur dat omhoog komt minder honger en snel een vol gevoel je opeens niet goed voelen na het eten (dit heet dumping) overgeven of opboeren na het eten moeite met slapen doordat je half zittend moet slapen moeite met slikken of het gevoel dat het eten niet goed zakt. Hier kun je ook last van hebben als je niet geopereerd bent, maar alleen chemotherapie met bestraling hebt gehad. Pijn. Eten kan pijn doen na de behandeling. Soms heeft de pijn geen duidelijke oorzaak . Moe zijn. Na de behandeling kun je heel moe zijn. Dit kan heel lang duren. Uitrusten helpt meestal niet. Soms is er een lichamelijke oorzaak. Ook door pijn en psychische klachten kun je erg moe worden. Ook na de behandeling ben je in je hoofd veel met je ziekte bezig. Je bent misschien bang dat de kanker weer terugkomt. Of je bent somber of verdrietig . Dat kan ook komen doordat je moet leren leven met een buismaag. Na de operatie duurt het vaak maanden of jaren voordat je weer fitter bent. Je kunt hier op veel plekken hulp bij krijgen. Een diëtist kan je tips geven over hoe en wat je het beste kunt eten met een buismaag. Voor sommige klachten door een buismaag kun je tijdelijk medicijnen krijgen. Bijvoorbeeld tegen maagzuur en diarree. Soms zakt je eten niet goed. Dat kan komen doordat de plek waar de slokdarm aan de buismaag vastzit te smal is. Of door littekens van bestraling, als je geen operatie hebt gehad. De arts kan zulke plekken voorzichtig wijder maken tijdens een kijk-onderzoek. Het is belangrijk dat je je klachten met je huisarts of de arts in het ziekenhuis bespreekt. De arts kan onderzoeken waar je klachten door komen. Soms is er een oplossing. Of lukt het je steeds beter om ermee om te gaan.

Testing for prostate cancer has advantages and disadvantages. patient_question: I'm worried about whether I might have prostate cancer
canonical_question: What treatment options do I have for my cancer?
english_summary:
• Prostate cancer usually does not cause any symptoms.
• Urinary complaints are not a sign of prostate cancer.
• Talk to your doctor if you are very concerned about prostate cancer.
• Do you have an error in the BRCA2 gene? Get tested for prostate cancer.
english_full: What is prostate cancer? Prostate cancer usually does not cause any symptoms. Problems with urination are almost never caused by prostate cancer. Prostate cancer almost never develops near the urethra. But precisely on the outside of the prostate. Cancer cells can then not press against your urethra. And therefore no problems with urination. Do you have problems urinating? Such as frequent urination, a weak stream, dripping or not being able to hold your pee properly? Look at problems with urination in men. Only when the prostate cancer has already grown far do men sometimes experience complaints. Complaints that men may experience include: losing weight without trying, if your prostate has become larger or has been removed: urinary complaints The cancer has also started to grow in other places in your body. These are metastases. In prostate cancer, cancer cells grow in the prostate. It mainly occurs in older men. 9 out of 10 men with prostate cancer are over 60 years old. There are 2 types of prostate cancer: This type of prostate cancer is the most common. This cancer is different from many other types of cancer: it is much less dangerous. He almost never gives complaints. Treatment is often not necessary. Especially not in older men.
original_dutch: Wat is prostaatkanker Prostaatkanker geeft meestal geen klachten. Problemen met plassen komen bijna nooit door prostaatkanker. En dus geen problemen met plassen geven. Heb je problemen met plassen? Zoals vaak plassen, een zwakke straal, nadruppelen of je plas niet goed kunnen ophouden? Kijk bij problemen met plassen bij mannen . Alleen als de prostaatkanker al ver gegroeid is, krijgen mannen daar soms klachten van. Klachten die mannen dan wel kunnen krijgen zijn bijvoorbeeld: afvallen zonder dat je dat probeert als je prostaat groter of weggehaald is: plasklachten De kanker is dan ook op andere plekken in je lichaam gaan groeien. Dit zijn uitzaaiingen. Bij prostaatkanker groeien er kankercellen in de prostaat. Het komt vooral voor bij oudere mannen. 9 van de 10 mannen met prostaatkanker zijn ouder dan 60 jaar. Er zijn 2 soorten prostaatkanker: Deze soort prostaatkanker komt het meeste voor. Deze kanker is anders dan veel andere soorten kanker: hij is veel minder gevaarlijk. Hij geeft bijna nooit klachten. Een behandeling is vaak niet nodig. Vooral niet bij oudere mannen.

english_full: What is prostate cancer? With prostate cancer, cancer cells grow in the prostate. It mainly occurs in older men. 9 out of 10 men with prostate cancer are over 60 years old. There are 2 types of prostate cancer: This type of prostate cancer is the most common. This cancer is different from many other types of cancer: it is much less dangerous. For example, because the cancer moves to other places in the body. Especially to the bones. That can hurt. Treatment is often necessary. patient_question: Testicular cancer
english_full: In testicular cancer, cancer cells grow in a testicle. Your testicle will then feel larger. Or there are lumps on it. If you have testicular cancer, you will have surgery right away. The doctor will then remove your testicle. Sometimes the cancer is not completely gone afterward. You can then receive radiation. Or chemotherapy. About 95 out of 100 men are cured of testicular cancer.
original_dutch: Bij zaadbalkanker groeien er kankercellen in een zaadbal. Je zaadbal voelt dan groter aan. Of er zitten knobbels op. Als je zaadbalkanker hebt, krijg je meteen een operatie.
